Introducing Dezzy, a second generation BMW 5 series. Born on 1st August 1987 in Germany and sold in London the same year. The second generation 5 Series is regarded by many as one of the best cars BMW ever made, and one of the most handsome cars on the road in the 80's with its alluring combination of quiet quality and refinement. The executive styling had a distinct BMW identity and was nicely proportioned and detailed inside and out.  The e28 was a staple on British roads throughout the 80s with over 7million being sold. 

Dezzy is one of very few red BMW e28's still alive and kicking, completely untouched and looking the same as the day he rolled off the production line. A 525e so one of the later higher spec'd editions with a 2.7l 6 cylinder and an immaculate black Houndstooth cloth interior. It’s a beautiful car that I take a lot of pride in owning ... and using, the body work and mechanics are close to perfect.

Introducing Frank, a 1979 BMW e23 -the original 7 series. Right from its launch in 1977, BMW’s Seven Series was always the more ‘techy’ and sporting choice amongst executive saloons. A very handsome car and survivors are very vary rare these days, particularly ones in good condition as Frank. He has been extensively restored and repainted in his original Fjord blue colour, which looks stunning (if I do say so myself). The interior is original and very unique in contrast to the usually stale interiors of bmws. Clad in a rich blue velour and accessories with wood vaneer. A lovely big handsome car that I take huge pride in owning and driving.

From the prolific drawing board of Barry Stimson, although only in his early twenties Barry designed & built the moulds together with a prototype single-handed in only a few short weeks

Underneath the attractive fibreglass bodywork is a space frame chassis, a Mini front subframe & new 1293cc MG metro engine, Together with two sets of Mini rear trailing arms

This particular car is a Mk 2, one of only three made and was the last car to be moulded before Barry sold on the project that sadly was never seen again 

This MK2 Safari Six took part in the very first Italian Job to Italy in 1990 & appears in the video ‘The Great Italian Caper’ & has attended many international mini meetings since Mini 25 in 1984

I first saw this car on Tomorrows world in 1972 when I was 12, I have owned this car along with the original prototype for the last 45 years 

Approximately 10 examples of the Six were built in total

Introducing Dez, a second generation BMW 5 series. Born on 1st August 1987 in Germany and sold in London the same year. The second generation 5 Series is regarded by many as one of the best cars BMW ever made, and one of the most handsome cars on the road in the 80's with its alluring combination of quiet quality and refinement. The executive styling had a distinct BMW identity and was nicely proportioned and detailed inside and out. The e28 was a staple on British roads throughout the 80s with over 7million being sold. Ella is one of very few red BMW e28's still alive and kicking, completely untouched and looking the same as the day she rolled off the production line. A 525e so one of the later higher spec'd editions with a 2.7l 6 cylinder and an immaculate black cloth interior. It’s a beautiful car that I take a lot of pride in owning ... and using, the body work is close to perfect and otherwise is in very very good condition.

Introducing Leroy, a first generation Volkswagen Golf born 1st December 1982 in Wolfsburg and sold to a Londoner that same year. Styled by Giorgetto Giugiaro, Leroy is a beautiful example of the iconic successor to the Volkswagen Beetle. The first generation Golf was a staple fixture on British roads throughout the late 70's and 80's. A beautifully simple design made for your average man or woman to roam the roads and by 1983 Volkswagen had sold over 5million. Leroy is one of very few Gold edition first generation golfs. Completely untouched and looking the same as the day she rolled off the production line. A GL (Second in the range to a GTI) so a high spec edition with a 1.5, 8v, 70 horsepower engine and beautiful beige/brown cloth interior. It’s a beautiful car that I take a lot of pride in owning ... and using, she has a few minor dings but generally in very very good condition.
